plainspeak.doctor: A defined contract enabling software components to communicate via structured requests and responses over a network or within a process boundary.
plainspeak.high-school: A set of rules that lets one program talk to another program, like a waiter taking your order to the kitchen.
plainspeak.kindergarten: A way for computer programs to ask each other for things.

plainspeak.doctor: A text-based interface for interacting with a computer system by issuing commands as lines of text, typically through a shell or terminal emulator.
plainspeak.high-school: A way to control a computer by typing commands instead of clicking buttons.
plainspeak.kindergarten: Talking to a computer by typing words.

plainspeak.doctor: The primary processor in a computer that executes instructions from programs through fetch-decode-execute cycles, containing arithmetic-logic and control units.
plainspeak.high-school: The brain of a computer that does all the calculations and runs your programs.
plainspeak.kindergarten: The part of a computer that does the thinking.

plainspeak.doctor: A data structure that allows multiple users to edit concurrently without coordination, automatically resolving conflicts through mathematically guaranteed merge semantics.
plainspeak.high-school: A way for multiple people to edit the same document at the same time without getting conflicts, even if they're offline.
plainspeak.kindergarten: A trick that lets two people write on the same page at the same time without messing it up.

plainspeak.doctor: NVIDIA's parallel computing platform that enables general-purpose computation on GPUs, allowing thousands of threads to execute concurrently.
plainspeak.high-school: A technology that lets programmers use graphics cards to do math really fast, which is great for AI training.
plainspeak.kindergarten: A special way to make the powerful part of a graphics card do super-fast calculations.

plainspeak.doctor: A finite directed graph with no directed cycles, representing a partially ordered set of nodes where edges define dependencies and topological ordering is always possible.
plainspeak.high-school: A flowchart where arrows go one way and you can never loop back to where you started. Used to manage task dependencies.
plainspeak.kindergarten: A drawing of connected boxes where the arrows never come back to the start.

plainspeak.doctor: A specialized processor with thousands of cores designed for parallel computation, originally for rendering graphics but now essential for AI workloads.
plainspeak.high-school: A powerful chip originally made for games that turned out to be amazing for AI because it can do many calculations at once.
plainspeak.kindergarten: A super-fast calculator chip that helps AI learn.

plainspeak.doctor: A comprehensive software application combining a source code editor, build automation tools, debugger, and often language server support into a single interface.
plainspeak.high-school: An all-in-one app for writing code that includes a text editor, tools to run your code, and ways to find bugs.
plainspeak.kindergarten: A special program for writing computer programs.

plainspeak.doctor: A lightweight, language-independent data interchange format using human-readable key-value pairs and arrays, defined by ECMA-404 and RFC 8259.
plainspeak.high-school: A way to write data using curly braces and colons that's easy for both humans and computers to read.
plainspeak.kindergarten: A way to write down information that computers can understand.

plainspeak.doctor: A neural network with billions to trillions of parameters trained auto-regressively on massive text corpora, capable of generating coherent text and performing in-context learning.
plainspeak.high-school: A computer program that has read billions of pages of text and can write essays, answer questions, and have conversations.
plainspeak.kindergarten: A super smart computer that can talk and write like a person.

plainspeak.doctor: A protocol (JSON-RPC based) that standardizes communication between a code editor and a language server, providing features like autocomplete, go-to-definition, and diagnostics.
plainspeak.high-school: A standard way for code editors to get smart features like auto-complete and error checking by talking to a background program.
plainspeak.kindergarten: A helper that makes coding programs better at guessing what you are trying to type.

plainspeak.doctor: An open protocol standardized by Anthropic that defines how LLMs connect to external tools, data sources, and services through a unified server-client architecture.
plainspeak.high-school: A standard way for AI models to plug into tools and databases, like a USB port for AI capabilities.
plainspeak.kindergarten: A way to plug AI into other computer programs so it can do more things.

plainspeak.doctor: A subset of AI where algorithms improve through experience by identifying patterns in data, typically via statistical models trained on labeled or unlabeled datasets.
plainspeak.high-school: Teaching a computer to learn from examples instead of giving it step-by-step instructions.
plainspeak.kindergarten: Showing a computer lots of examples so it can learn to do things on its own.

plainspeak.doctor: An NLP task that identifies and classifies named entities (persons, organizations, locations, dates, etc.) in unstructured text into predefined categories.
plainspeak.high-school: A computer skill that picks out names, places, and dates from text and labels what they are.
plainspeak.kindergarten: Teaching a computer to find people's names and place names in stories.

plainspeak.doctor: A subfield of AI focused on the interaction between computers and human language, encompassing tasks like parsing, generation, translation, and sentiment analysis.
plainspeak.high-school: Technology that helps computers understand, process, and generate human language like English.
plainspeak.kindergarten: Teaching computers to understand what people say and write.

plainspeak.doctor: A technique that enhances LLM outputs by first retrieving relevant documents from a knowledge base and conditioning the model's generation on that retrieved context.
plainspeak.high-school: A method where an AI looks up facts in a database before answering, like checking your notes before a test.
plainspeak.kindergarten: Letting the AI peek at a book for the right answer before it speaks.

plainspeak.doctor: A fine-tuning paradigm where a reward model trained on human preference data guides policy optimization of an LLM using reinforcement learning.
plainspeak.high-school: A training method where humans rate AI outputs, and the AI learns to produce better answers based on those ratings.
plainspeak.kindergarten: Teaching an AI by telling it which answers are good and which are bad, like giving a gold star or a frown.

plainspeak.doctor: A collection of libraries, tools, documentation, and sample code that enables developers to build applications for a specific platform or service.
plainspeak.high-school: A toolkit with code libraries and instructions that helps programmers build apps for a specific platform.
plainspeak.kindergarten: A box of tools for building programs that work with a particular service.

plainspeak.doctor: A target metric (e.g., 99.9% uptime) defining the desired reliability level for a service, used with SLIs and SLAs to manage operational risk.
plainspeak.high-school: A promise about how reliable a service will be, like 'this website will work 99.9% of the time.'
plainspeak.kindergarten: A goal for how often a service should work without breaking.

plainspeak.doctor: A cryptographic network protocol for operating services securely over an unsecured network, providing encrypted remote login and command execution.
plainspeak.high-school: A secure way to log into another computer over the internet and run commands on it.
plainspeak.kindergarten: A secret tunnel to another computer so you can use it from far away.

plainspeak.doctor: A cryptographic protocol providing end-to-end encryption, authentication, and integrity for communications over a computer network, the successor to SSL.
plainspeak.high-school: The technology that keeps your internet traffic private by encrypting it so nobody can spy on it.
plainspeak.kindergarten: A secret code that keeps what you see on the internet safe from snoops.

plainspeak.doctor: Google's custom ASIC designed specifically for accelerating machine learning workloads, optimized for TensorFlow operations with matrix arithmetic.
plainspeak.high-school: A special chip Google made just for AI calculations, even faster than a GPU for certain tasks.
plainspeak.kindergarten: A super-fast computer chip made just for AI.

plainspeak.doctor: The point of human-computer interaction comprising visual elements, controls, and feedback mechanisms that enable a user to operate a software application.
plainspeak.high-school: The screens, buttons, and menus you see and click on when using an app or website.
plainspeak.kindergarten: The pictures and buttons you see on a screen that you can tap or click.

plainspeak.doctor: The overall quality of interaction a person has with a system, encompassing usability, accessibility, aesthetics, and emotional response throughout the user journey.
plainspeak.high-school: How easy and pleasant it feels to use a website or app.
plainspeak.kindergarten: How much fun and easy it is to use something.

VLLM.expanded: Virtual Large Language Model / vLLM Engine

plainspeak.doctor: A high-throughput, memory-efficient serving engine for LLMs featuring PagedAttention, continuous batching, and tensor parallelism for production inference.
plainspeak.high-school: A system that runs AI models efficiently and handles lots of users at the same time without running out of memory.
plainspeak.kindergarten: A smart program that lets lots of people use a big AI at the same time.

plainspeak.doctor: A compatibility layer in Windows that enables running Linux binary executables natively through a Linux kernel interface provided by Microsoft.
plainspeak.high-school: A way to run Linux programs directly on Windows without needing a separate computer or virtual machine.
plainspeak.kindergarten: Lets you use Linux programs on a Windows computer.

plainspeak.doctor: A human-readable data serialization language using indentation-based structure, commonly used for configuration files in CI/CD and application deployments.
plainspeak.high-school: A way to write configuration files using indentation (spaces) that's easy for humans to read and edit.
plainspeak.kindergarten: A way to write settings for programs using spaces and dashes.

plainspeak.doctor: A real-time object detection system that frames detection as a single regression problem, directly predicting bounding boxes and class probabilities from full images in one pass.
plainspeak.high-school: An AI system that can spot objects in images or video in real time by looking at the whole picture at once.
plainspeak.kindergarten: A clever computer program that can find things in pictures really fast.
